title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox lost 8-6 to the Twins at Fenway Park after leading for most of the game.
Justin Slaten struggled in the 7th inning, giving up four runs and two home runs.
Payton Tolle recorded nine strikeouts before being relieved after six innings on 85 pitches.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the ceremonial first pitch at Fenway Park ahead of the Red Sox versus Twins game.
Clemens, 63, pitched to his son Kody, currently playing for the Minnesota Twins, wearing a custom Red Sox jersey.
The Red Sox distributed Clemens bobbleheads and he signed autographs outside Fenway Park before the event.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White was named to the NBA All-Defensive First Team for the 2025-26 season with 58 votes.
White recorded a career-high 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading all guards with 552 contested shots.
Boston Celtics ended the regular season with a 56-26 record but exited in the first playoff round.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed,' starring Tatiana Maslany, is now streaming on Apple TV.
Oscar-winning Civil War drama 'Glory' is available on Netflix until June 1.
French animated film 'Arco,' featuring a star-studded English dub, streams on Hulu.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Garrett Crochet will have a throwing session this weekend, followed by live batting practice next week.
Roman Anthony remains sidelined due to pain when swinging, with no new developments from specialists.
Trevor Story will miss 6-10 weeks after sports hernia surgery, impacting the Red Sox lineup.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox discussing early trade talks for a hitter due to offensive struggles and injuries.
Boston ranks second-last in MLB for runs scored and near last in home runs, prompting trade urgency.
Key injured players include Trevor Story; team swept Royals but seeks consistency against Twins.

title: Mock draft predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan star in first round
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Red Sox projected to pick Bishop Feehan's Brody Bumila at No. 20 in MLB draft, per MLB.com's Jonathan Mayo.
Bumila, a 6-foot-9 left-hander, known for high-octane fastballs, is rising on draft boards despite limited offspeed pitches.
Scouts attended Bumila's recent game; opinions vary on draft positioning, but potential upside attracts first-round attention.

title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story underwent successful hernia surgery and is expected to miss 6-10 weeks.
Story, 33, has faced multiple injuries with the Red Sox, including surgeries on his shoulder and elbow.
Isaiah Kiner-Falefa, Andruw Monasterio, and Nick Sogard are filling in at shortstop during Story's absence.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design, decades after leaving art school for music.
Wolf, frontman for J. Geils Band, recounted his early days college-hopping and shared upcoming plans for an album and book.
Wolf reflects on his art and music careers, mentioning influences like German Expressionists and stories from his memoir 'Waiting on the Moon.'

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh joins Boston 25 as a sports media journalist, filling a newly created position.
Walsh, a Medford native and Syracuse graduate, previously worked at WSMV in Nashville for three years.
She expressed excitement about covering Boston teams and joining the Boston 25 team she grew up watching.
